channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Cointelegraph - Security, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading, Blockthreat, CryptoPotato, CryptoSlate, Krebs On Security, The Hacker News, Coindesk, GroupIB Blog, Data Breach Today, CNN.com - Crypto News, DataBreaches.net

item.title: Bitcoin price shows resilience above $60,000 amid renewed US-Iran hostilities
item.description: Bitcoin price held above $62,000 after renewed fighting between the United States and Iran slowed traffic through the Strait of Hormuz and sent oil prices higher, reviving inflation concerns across gl...
item.dc:creator: CryptoSlate

item.description: Microsoft has announced plans to disable Outlook Web Access (OWA) Light, the lightweight version of the Outlook Web App email client, in a future Exchange Server update. [...]...

item.description: Everyone seems to have announced a clearinghouse over the past few weeks. We did too. Ours is called Athena, and the main thing that sets it apart is that it was already real and running when we annou...

item.description: These options settle into underlying futures contracts rather than spot cryptoassets, involving no custody, transfer, or administration of tokens....

item.title: Bitcoin’s bottom needs long-term holders to stop losing $280M a day
item.description: Bitcoin's climb from $58,300 to $64,400, then back to $62,700, over the past week is a bounce that still leaves the price below two important levels tracked by Glassnode: the True Market Mean near $76...
item.dc:creator: CryptoSlate

item.title: GodDamn Ransomware Uses PoisonX Driver to Disable Endpoint Defenses
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new ransomware family called GodDamn that employs the PoisonX kernel driver to neutralize security software as part of its defense evasion strategy.
According...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Crypto remains resilient in face of renewed Middle East tensions
item.description: BTC rose 1.2% to $63,000 and Nasdaq futures jumped 2.6% with markets seemingly unconcerned by U.S. airstrikes on Iran. Bitcoin is now up 9% since the end of June....
item.dc:creator: Coindesk

item.title: Top AI Agents Built to Catch Malicious Code Can Be Tricked Into Running It
item.description:
Ask an AI coding agent to scan open-source code for security holes, and it might run the attacker's code on your own machine instead.
That is the finding in a proof-of-concept published Wednesday by ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: GitHub 'Verified' Commits Can Be Rewritten Into New Hashes Without Breaking Signatures

item.title: RedWing MaaS Packages Android Bank Fraud as a Telegram Rental Service
item.description: A new Android malware operation called RedWing is being rented out on Telegram as a ready-made bank-fraud service. It lets even low-skill criminals take over a victim's phone, steal their banking logi...

item.title: Rogue Agent Flaw Could Have Let Attackers Hijack Google Dialogflow CX Chatbots
item.description:
A critical flaw in Google's Dialogflow CX could have let an attacker with edit rights on one Code Block-enabled agent compromise other Code Block-enabled agents in the same Google Cloud project.
From...
